data.title: Update project docs to reflect DSO artifact auto-update mechanism
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Update internal project documentation (CLAUDE.md) to reflect the new artifact auto-update system introduced by this epic.

**Why**: After S1–S3 ship, CLAUDE.md's Quick Reference table and Architecture section will be stale — they won't mention `dso update-artifacts`, the SessionStart hook, or the version stamp mechanism. Future agents and developers reading CLAUDE.md will miss these capabilities.

**Scope**:
- IN: CLAUDE.md Quick Reference table: add `dso update-artifacts` row
- IN: CLAUDE.md Architecture / Hook architecture section: mention `check-artifact-versions.sh` as a SessionStart hook and its silent-exit guard for plugin source repos
- IN: CLAUDE.md Architecture section: note that installed artifacts carry a `# dso-version:` / `x-dso-version:` stamp and that `dso update-artifacts` is the update mechanism
- OUT: Creating new documentation files
- OUT: User-facing docs (INSTALL.md) — covered by S4

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, CLAUDE.md Quick Reference table includes a `dso update-artifacts` row with its purpose
  ← Satisfies: "Internal docs reflect new commands introduced by the epic"
- When this story is complete, CLAUDE.md Architecture section documents the `check-artifact-versions.sh` SessionStart hook, including the 24h cache, version-change bust, and plugin-source-repo silent-exit guard
  ← Satisfies: "Internal docs reflect new hook architecture introduced by the epic"
- When this story is complete, CLAUDE.md accurately reflects the version stamp mechanism used in installed artifacts
  ← Satisfies: "Internal docs reflect new architecture introduced by the epic"
- When this story is complete, all CLAUDE.md documentation updates are consistent with the implemented S1–S3 behavior (no phantom flags or stale descriptions)
  ← Satisfies: "Internal docs are accurate and usable by future agents"

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Follow `.claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md` for formatting, structure, and conventions
- Follow `.claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md` for documentation formatting, structure, and conventions.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
